query: Can we build a vector or dense index over A-box instance data?
notes: current: dense A-box allowed only as a bounded seed; stale: outright ban

query: Do local models hit an execution ceiling on multi-module coding tasks?
notes: current: no ceiling, injection enabled the cache-gap fix; stale: ceiling exists

query: Is the value of push/injection memory delivery proven for local models?
notes: current: evidence-supported; stale: built but value unproven

query: Do local models reliably pull memory on their own without push?
notes: current: pull gap is real, push closes it

query: Does the relate() primitive validate its endpoints?
notes: relate validates subject/object against SHACL shapes

query: Why does MOOSEDev use a shared backend over a Unix socket instead of per-client stdio?
notes: single-writer RocksDB lock -> shared backend for concurrent clients

query: What is the convention for record titles or labels?
notes: rdfs:label = short designator handle, the claim goes in the description

query: Is moose search_records flat BM25 or field-weighted?
What does recall-first mean before recording into the graph?
notes: recall-first = a list-all get_relevant_context, not a topic probe

query: What happens to the dense index after a direct write to the store?
notes: direct writes bypass index_record -> dense index stale -> refresh

query: Did the hybrid dense seed help end-to-end currency answering after the label contract?
Why is the project knowledge graph the source of truth instead of markdown files?
notes: structured typed knowledge over free text (invariant #2)

query: What rationale supports adding an in-place status/retract transition for knowledge records?
notes: traverse hasRationale of the retract/status requirement

notes: bounded-seed constraint supersedes the no-index/ban constraints
